title: Founding of Anuradhapura
description: King Pandukabhaya establishes Anuradhapura as the capital of a unified Sinhalese kingdom.
realOutcome: Created the political and urban foundation for over a millennium of Sri Lankan civilization.

title: Arrival of Arahat Mahinda
description: Arahat Mahinda arrives in Mihintale, introducing Buddhism to King Devanampiya Tissa.
realOutcome: Buddhism became the state religion, fundamentally shaping Sri Lankan culture.

title: Battle of Vijithapura
description: King Dutugemunu defeats King Elara in a massive battle to reunite the island.
realOutcome: The island was unified under a single Sri Lankan ruler.

title: Construction of Ruwanwelisaya
description: King Dutugemunu completes the Ruwanwelisaya stupa, one of the world's tallest ancient structures.
realOutcome: Became a sacred Buddhist monument and symbol of royal devotion for centuries.

title: Sigiriya Rock Fortress
description: King Kashyapa builds his palace atop Sigiriya and adorns the rock with famed frescoes.
realOutcome: Created an iconic fortress-palace, though Kashyapa was later defeated and the site abandoned as capital.

title: The Chola Invasion
description: Raja Raja Chola I invades, capturing King Mahinda V and destroying the capital.
realOutcome: Led to the fall of Anuradhapura and the shifting of the capital to Polonnaruwa.

title: Vijayabahu I Liberates Polonnaruwa
description: King Vijayabahu I drives out the Cholas and re-establishes Sinhalese rule from Polonnaruwa.
realOutcome: Restored indigenous kingship and began Polonnaruwa's era as the island's capital.

title: Reign of Parakramabahu the Great
description: King Parakramabahu unifies the country and begins constructing the massive Parakrama Samudra.
realOutcome: A golden age of agriculture where 'not a single drop of water reached the sea without serving man.'

title: Nissanka Malla's Royal Inscriptions
description: King Nissanka Malla commissions the Gal Pota and other monuments celebrating just rule.
realOutcome: Left enduring stone records of Polonnaruwa's cultural and legal ideals.

title: Pandya Invasion of Polonnaruwa
description: South Indian Pandya forces raid the kingdom, weakening Sinhalese control of the north.
realOutcome: Exposed the kingdom's vulnerability to repeated South Indian incursions.

title: Invasion of Kalinga Magha
description: A brutal invasion by Kalinga Magha destroys Polonnaruwa's infrastructure and religious sites.
realOutcome: Forced the Sinhalese kingdoms to migrate to the southwestern parts of the island.

title: Portuguese Land in Colombo
description: Lourenço de Almeida's fleet arrives, establishing Portugal's foothold on the coast.
realOutcome: Opened centuries of European colonial competition for control of Sri Lanka.

title: Battle of Danture
description: Kandyan forces under Vimaladharmasuriya I annihilate the invading Portuguese army.
realOutcome: Secured Kandy's independence and established it as a major stronghold against colonizers.

title: Matara Rebellion Against the Dutch
description: Kandyan and low-country resistance rises against Dutch East India Company rule.
realOutcome: Weakened Dutch control but failed to expel European powers from the island.

title: British Replace the Dutch
description: Britain seizes Dutch coastal territories during the Napoleonic Wars.
realOutcome: Transferred colonial dominance to Britain, setting the stage for unified colonial rule.

title: The Kandyan Convention
description: Kandyan chieftains sign a treaty with the British, deposing King Sri Vikrama Rajasinha.
realOutcome: The entire island fell under British colonial rule, ending over 2000 years of Sinhalese monarchy.

title: Uva-Wellassa Rebellion
description: A widespread uprising against British rule erupts in the Uva and Wellassa regions.
realOutcome: Brutally suppressed, devastating the region and consolidating British authority.

title: Assassination of Archduke Franz Ferdinand
description: Archduke Franz Ferdinand of Austria and his wife Sophie are shot dead in Sarajevo by Gavrilo Princip.
realOutcome: Led directly to the outbreak of World War I.

title: Sinking of the Lusitania
description: A German U-boat torpedoes the British ocean liner RMS Lusitania, killing 1,198 people.
realOutcome: Shifted public opinion in the US against Germany, leading to their eventual entry into the war.

title: Battle of the Somme
description: Allied forces launch a massive offensive on the Western Front with catastrophic casualties.
realOutcome: Symbolized the industrial slaughter of trench warfare and failed to break the stalemate.

title: The Zimmermann Telegram
description: British intelligence intercepts a secret German proposal for a military alliance with Mexico.
realOutcome: Forced the United States to abandon neutrality and declare war on Germany.

title: Russian Revolution
description: The Bolsheviks seize power in Russia, taking the empire out of the war.
realOutcome: Ended Russia's participation and reshaped global politics for the 20th century.

title: Treaty of Versailles
description: The Allied powers and Germany sign a peace treaty imposing heavy reparations and territorial losses.
realOutcome: Ended the war but created economic and political instability that fueled the rise of Nazi Germany.

title: Assassination of Julius Caesar
description: Senators stab Julius Caesar to death on the Ides of March in the Theatre of Pompey.
realOutcome: Plunged Rome into civil war and ended the Roman Republic, paving the way for the Empire.

title: Battle of Actium
description: Octavian defeats Mark Antony and Cleopatra's fleet off the coast of Greece.
realOutcome: Made Octavian master of Rome and established the foundation of the Roman Empire.

title: Great Fire of Rome
description: A devastating fire burns much of Rome; Nero undertakes massive rebuilding projects.
realOutcome: Transformed the city's architecture and became a lasting symbol of Nero's controversial reign.

title: Edict of Milan
description: Emperors Constantine and Licinius legalize Christianity across the Roman Empire.
realOutcome: Christianity spread rapidly and reshaped European culture and politics for centuries.

title: Founding of Constantinople
description: Constantine dedicates Byzantium as Nova Roma, the empire's new eastern capital.
realOutcome: Shifted imperial gravity eastward and prolonged Roman institutions for another millennium.

title: Fall of the Western Roman Empire
description: Germanic chieftain Odoacer deposes the last Western Roman emperor, Romulus Augustulus.
realOutcome: Marked the conventional end of ancient Rome and the start of the medieval period in the West.
